codeReview.summary: Current HEAD keeps the feature fix from `28eafbd2f437f7f299401b68e09f8b6eb8a71a59` intact. `CreditLimitRequestController.createRequest` now resolves dealer sessions through `DealerPortalService`, explicitly denies preserved non-active dealers before request creation, and therefore closes the previously missed canonical write surface on `POST /api/v1/credit/limit-requests`. `PortalFinanceControllerIT` now proves the intended contract end-to-end: a held dealer can still read ledger and aging, but both broader dealer-portal access (`/api/v1/dealer-portal/dashboard`) and the canonical credit-limit write route return `403`. I did not find any new high-confidence blocking correctness issues in scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission guidance describes the restriction mostly in terms of blocking broader `dealer-portal` access, but dealer-capable routes are not fully namespaced under `/api/v1/dealer-portal/**`. Workers hardening non-active dealer access need an explicit route inventory, not just prefix-based guarding assumptions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission `AGENTS.md:34,55` requires finance-read-only access for non-active dealer logins, while the canonical dealer write route lives at `erp-domain/src/main/java/com/bigbrightpaints/erp/modules/sales/controller/CreditLimitRequestController.java:28,47-69` (`/api/v1/credit/limit-requests`) outside the dealer-portal prefix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo's Maven invocation is cwd-sensitive because `.mvn/settings.xml` is resolved relative to `erp-domain`, but that constraint was still easy for the worker to miss during baseline proof execution.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The feature handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` and notes that an initial `-f .../pom.xml` run failed because `erp-domain/.mvn/settings.xml` is cwd-relative, then suggests documenting that explicitly (`.../handoffs/2026-04-23T04-18-35-360Z__shared-dealer-master-preservation-hardcut__1c3ce5fa-9a14-4d59-b5a0-907340350431.json:71-80`).

summary: Pass: the follow-up fix closes the prior blocking gap by denying non-active dealer sessions on `POST /api/v1/credit/limit-requests` while preserving finance read-only dealer access, and I found no new blocking correctness issues in scope.
